extra_dsl_fields.ofi: Daily order-flow imbalance from signed tick volume.
extra_dsl_fields.realized_vol: Intraday realized volatility sampled at one-minute intervals.
extra_dsl_fields.days_to_next_cornerstone_lockup: Days to the next cornerstone lockup expiry.
extra_dsl_fields.next_cornerstone_unlock_pct_offer: Next cornerstone unlock as a share of the offer.
extra_dsl_fields.days_since_prev_cornerstone_lockup: Days since the previous cornerstone lockup expiry.
extra_dsl_fields.next_cornerstone_unlock_shares: Shares in the next cornerstone unlock.
extra_dsl_fields.next_cornerstone_unlock_pct_cap: Next cornerstone unlock as a share of market cap.
extra_dsl_fields.days_to_next_greenshoe_expiry: Days to the next greenshoe expiry.
extra_dsl_fields.days_to_next_stabilization_end: Days to the next stabilization end.
extra_dsl_fields.days_since_prev_greenshoe_expiry: Days since the previous greenshoe expiry.
extra_dsl_fields.days_to_next_greenshoe_exercise: Days to the next greenshoe exercise.
extra_dsl_fields.days_since_prev_greenshoe_exercise: Days since the previous greenshoe exercise.
extra_dsl_fields.days_since_prev_stabilization_end: Days since the previous stabilization end.
extra_dsl_fields.days_since_prev_stabilization_start: Days since the previous stabilization start.
extra_dsl_fields.is_pre_greenshoe_expiry_5d: Indicator for the five days before greenshoe expiry.
extra_dsl_fields.is_pre_cornerstone_lockup_5d: Indicator for the five days before a cornerstone unlock.
extra_dsl_fields.is_near_greenshoe_expiry_5d: Indicator for a five-day greenshoe-expiry neighborhood.
extra_dsl_fields.is_near_greenshoe_exercise_5d: Indicator for a five-day greenshoe-exercise neighborhood.
extra_dsl_fields.is_near_cornerstone_lockup_5d: Indicator for a five-day cornerstone-unlock neighborhood.
extra_dsl_fields.is_pre_stabilization_end_5d: Indicator for the five days before stabilization ends.
extra_dsl_fields.is_near_stabilization_end_5d: Indicator for a five-day stabilization-end neighborhood.
extra_dsl_fields.is_stabilization_window_active: Indicator for an active stabilization window.
extra_dsl_fields.first_hour_spread_shock: First-hour spread relative to its lagged baseline.

data_gaps.name: nonpositive_tick_values
data_gaps.evidence: Latest doctor found 364,768 nonpositive tick value rows across 77 stocks.
data_gaps.recommended_action: Keep value > 0 filters in derived SQL; add a source-level QA extract by stock/date/event_type before building intraday features.

data_gaps.name: event_terms_needs_review
data_gaps.recommended_action: Prioritize rows that affect greenshoe, stabilization, cornerstone, lockup, and tranche unlock dates; promote only source-backed terms.

data_gaps.name: bloomberg_lockup_anomalies
data_gaps.evidence: Bloomberg still reports anomalous lockup fields for 06051 and 03636; HKEX/prospectus terms should be treated as truth.
data_gaps.recommended_action: Use Bloomberg lockup fields only as review candidates unless confirmed by HKEX/prospectus source text.

data_gaps.name: raw_tick_intraday_loop_gap
data_gaps.evidence: The validation loop consumes daily bars plus tick-derived daily features; raw tick loading exists but the agent does not yet generate intraday features itself.
data_gaps.recommended_action: Add an intraday feature-build step that materializes opening-auction, first-hour, and event-window features from TRADE/BID/ASK ticks.

operator_constraints: Use /Users/caizhuoang/AlphaAgent as the official working tree.
Prefer HKEX/prospectus-derived event dates over Bloomberg lockup fields.
Do not treat promoted lenient-regime factors as production alpha without stricter follow-up.
director_context.plan_notes: Director selected an event-conditioned microstructure run because the core daily, microstructure, tick-manifest, and curated-event datasets are aligned. Data-review topics remain in the queue because they affect truth labeling and future intraday feature work.

director_topics.theme: HK IPO continuous event-decay microstructure signals
director_topics.rationale: The aligned daily, microstructure, and curated event tables can already support continuous event-distance interactions without collapsing the daily cross-section to a narrow Boolean event window.
director_topics.extra_guidance: The previous hard-window search produced 18/18 rejections because Boolean event gates collapsed the cross-section. Every proposal must retain a daily base microstructure signal and add a continuous interaction using event_decay(days_to_next_*, half_life), with half_life in {5, 10, 20}. Use OFI, relative spread, realized volatility, trade count, quote count, or average trade size. Do not multiply by is_pre_*, is_near_*, or is_stabilization_window_active as the sole signal. Do not use first_hour_* fields because this loader is daily-only. Avoid Bloomberg-only anomalous lockup dates. Prefer forms such as base_rank + event_decay(distance, 10) * interaction_rank so non-event stocks remain in the cross-section.
validation_args: HK IPO continuous event-decay microstructure signals

The previous hard-window search produced 18/18 rejections because Boolean event gates collapsed the cross-section. Every proposal must retain a daily base microstructure signal and add a continuous interaction using event_decay(days_to_next_*, half_life), with half_life in {5, 10, 20}. Use OFI, relative spread, realized volatility, trade count, quote count, or average trade size. Do not multiply by is_pre_*, is_near_*, or is_stabilization_window_active as the sole signal. Do not use first_hour_* fields because this loader is daily-only. Avoid Bloomberg-only anomalous lockup dates. Prefer forms such as base_rank + event_decay(distance, 10) * interaction_rank so non-event stocks remain in the cross-section.
success_criteria: At least one promoted factor survives lenient validation and holdout checks.
Promoted factors contain a continuous event-decay interaction while retaining a non-event daily base signal.
Failure breakdown identifies whether the next bottleneck is IC, rank IC, holdout, or turnover.
stop_conditions: Stop repeating this topic if promoted factors are generic or fail holdout repeatedly.
Switch to event data review if new failures trace to missing or ambiguous event labels.

director_topics.theme: HK IPO event truth and document review
director_topics.rationale: The event tables are usable and document coverage is complete, but 280 needs-review rows still require a deterministic backlog audit.
director_topics.extra_guidance: Use the data gaps to decide which HKEX/prospectus terms to review next. Prioritize missing or ambiguous greenshoe, stabilization, cornerstone, and lockup terms that affect the validation window.
success_criteria: Needs-review rows that affect validation are resolved or marked unavailable.
Bloomberg anomalies remain excluded from truth tables unless source-confirmed.
stop_conditions: Pause factor validation only if event truth gaps become blocking for selected dates.

director_topics.theme: HK IPO implementability and cost realism
director_topics.rationale: Early lenient promotions are useful for exploration, but production-quality research needs post-cost, turnover, and holdout stress before any alpha claim.
director_topics.extra_guidance: Stress previously promoted event-conditioned factors for turnover, spread, liquidity, holdout decay, and post-cost quantile spread. Prefer lower-turnover variants that remain stable outside the first listing week.
validation_args: HK IPO implementability and cost realism

Stress previously promoted event-conditioned factors for turnover, spread, liquidity, holdout decay, and post-cost quantile spread. Prefer lower-turnover variants that remain stable outside the first listing week.
success_criteria: Promoted candidates retain positive post-cost quantile spread.
Holdout rank IC does not flip sign.
Turnover is explainable by event timing rather than noisy daily churn.
stop_conditions: If all candidates fail cost or holdout gates, return to feature engineering.

director_topics.theme: HK IPO raw-tick intraday pressure features
director_topics.rationale: Tick-level TRADE/BID/ASK data is available, but the current validation loop mainly consumes daily materialized features. Intraday feature materialization is the next data-extension topic.
director_topics.extra_guidance: Design feature families that should be materialized from TRADE/BID/ASK ticks: first-hour OFI, opening-auction imbalance, quote-recovery speed, spread shock persistence, and event-window liquidity withdrawal.
success_criteria: A reproducible SQL/materialization plan exists for first-hour and event-window features.
Nonpositive tick rows are quantified by symbol/date/event_type before feature build.
stop_conditions: Do not use raw tick-derived intraday signals in validation until QA tables pass doctor checks.

hk_ipo_event_conditioned_microstructure.rationale: Event-conditioned microstructure produced promotions; next step is implementability, turnover, cost, and holdout stress rather than repeating the same candidate family.
No candidates promoted and failures point to missing metrics or event label sparsity; inspect event truth before more factor search.

hk_ipo_event_truth_review.rationale: Event-truth audit completed; stop the bounded run for deterministic review of blocking and backlog findings.

hk_ipo_cost_realism_oos.rationale: Promoted discovery candidates were replayed under the cost-stress contract; stop this bounded run and inspect the deterministic report.

hk_ipo_raw_tick_intraday_features.rationale: Raw-tick SQL was validated and dry-run with read-only QA; stop before any operator-approved BigQuery materialization.
